text: Dropshipping is a retail fulfillment method where the store doesn't keep products in stock. Instead, when a sale occurs, the store purchases the item from a third-party supplier who ships directly to the customer. The seller never handles the product — acting as a marketing and customer service layer.
Advantages: low upfront investment (no inventory), location independence, easy to test products.
Disadvantages: low margins (10-30%), no quality control, shipping times (often from China, 2-4 weeks), supplier reliability issues.
